data_in.description: GNPS feature-based molecular network file (GraphML or JSON)
MS2LDA substructural motif assignments (feature-to-motif mapping table)

data_out.description: Annotated feature-based molecular network with MS2LDA substructural motif annotations embedded as node attributes

domain_knowledge: MS2LDA motifs are probabilistic fragments derived from tandem mass spectra and represent recurring substructural patterns detectable across large datasets.
Feature-based molecular networking groups mass spectra by precursor m/z and spectral similarity, creating nodes for unique mass features rather than individual spectra.
pyMolNetEnhancer maps substructural information by matching MS2LDA motif IDs to feature node identifiers in the GNPS network graph.
Network annotations are stored as node attributes in GraphML or similar formats and can be visualized in network analysis software such as Cytoscape.

direct_checks: verify that pyMolNetEnhancer repository (madeleineernst/pyMolNetEnhancer) is accessible and contains implementation code for feature-based mapping
verify file_exists: a Python module or script within the repository that implements the feature-based MS2LDA-to-network mapping logic
verify script_runs: the feature-based mapping function accepts as inputs (1) a GNPS feature-based molecular network artifact and (2) MS2LDA substructural motif data, and executes without errors

verify output_matches_reference: the annotated network artifact produced contains node/edge attributes documenting which Mass2Motif substructures are mapped to which network features (robust to annotation format variations across GNPS versions)
verify file_format_is: the output network artifact is in a standard format compatible with GNPS (GraphML, JSON, or tabular edge/node list)
expert_review: Assess whether the feature-based mapping correctly associates MS2LDA motif presence/absence or intensity patterns with molecular network features (nodes), as distinct from the classical node-to-node or cluster-level mapping

Evaluate whether the annotated network artifact is semantically valid: substructure annotations should correspond to chemically plausible fragmentation patterns or structural motifs present in the underlying spectra
Assess reproducibility: confirm that running the feature-based mapping step deterministically reproduces the same annotated network when given identical inputs

cmd: import networkx as nx; import json; gnps_net = nx.read_graphml('{inputs.gnps_network}'); motifs = json.load(open('{inputs.ms2lda_motifs}')); nodes_updated = 0; [gnps_net.nodes[node].update({'ms2lda_motifs': motifs.get(node, [])}) for node in gnps_net.nodes() if node in motifs and (nodes_updated := nodes_updated + 1)]; nx.write_graphml(gnps_net, 'annotated_network_{task_id}.graphml'); print(f'Annotated {nodes_updated} nodes with MS2LDA motifs')

task_description: Map MS2LDA substructural motifs onto a GNPS feature-based molecular network using pyMolNetEnhancer to produce an annotated network artifact with substructural annotations.
task_objective: Map MS2LDA Mass2Motif substructural annotations onto a feature-based GNPS molecular network and produce an annotated network artifact.

title: Reconstruct the Map MS2LDA Substructural Information to Mass Spectral Molecular Network (Feature Based) component

workflow_description: 1. Load the GNPS feature-based molecular network file (GraphML or JSON format). 2. Load MS2LDA-derived substructural motif data mapping features to molecular fragments. 3. Execute pyMolNetEnhancer's feature-based mapping function to annotate network nodes with MS2LDA substructural information. 4. Merge motif annotations into the network graph, assigning substructure labels and confidence scores to corresponding features. 5. Export the annotated network in GraphML format with embedded substructural metadata.
